_doc: Fleet-baseline deny rails, keyed by channel mount — the centralized half of the gating engine (agent-core design, 2026-07-13, chunk B). Shipped with the canopy plugin so a rail fix propagates to every agent via /canopy:update. Each agent's hooks/gating_guard.py reads its config/gating.json `channels` list, pulls these rails for the mounted channels, substitutes {slug}, and merges them IN FRONT of the repo's own `deny` list at call time. ADD-ONLY: an agent's local config can add rails; it can never remove a baseline rail. Rails here must stay channel-generic — agent-specific rails belong in that agent's config/gating.json.

email.message: BLOCKED: raw `gog gmail send/reply` bypasses {slug}'s HTML wrapper and thread_id capture. Send via bin/{slug}-email (the shared canopy email engine) instead — write the body to a file and pass --body-file.

BLOCKED: `canopy email send --account` overrides {slug}'s repo identity — one mailbox per agent, never shared (identity bleed is the fleet's one hard rule). Send via bin/{slug}-email, which pins this repo's identity.

gws._pattern_doc: Deliverable filing rail (agent-core/deliverables.md). A converting `gog drive upload --convert` with NO `--parent` lands the Google Doc in the agent's personal My Drive root — invisible to the team, dead-linked to @dimagi.com recipients, and dies with the agent identity. Fires only on the deliverable path (upload + convert) so it can't false-positive on non-convert binary uploads or plain reads. Mounted by agents that publish via `gog drive upload`; agents whose helper always parents into a shared folder are compliant by construction and needn't mount it.
gws.message: BLOCKED: {slug} never creates a Google Doc in My Drive root. Every deliverable goes in a per-project subfolder under {slug}'s shared Projects root — pass `--parent <subfolderId>`. Reuse the project's existing folder or create the subfolder, then share it with the requester and confirm via `gog drive permissions`. See agent-core/deliverables.md.
